name: GitHub
description: Talk to this agent by @mentioning it in GitHub issue and pull-request comments — a GitHub App with webhook signature verification via eve's githubChannel.
setup:
This channel is a **GitHub App** the agent listens through — separate from any personal token an agent uses to *do* work.

1. Create a **GitHub App** (Settings → Developer settings → GitHub Apps). Note its **App ID** → `GITHUB_APP_ID` and its slug → `GITHUB_APP_SLUG` (the `@name` people mention).
2. Generate a **private key** (PEM) and put its full contents in `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`. Set a **webhook secret** → `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
3. Deploy the agent, then set the App's **Webhook URL** to `https://<your-domain>/e/<environmentId>/eve/v1/github`.
4. Under the App's webhook events, subscribe to **Issue comment** and **Pull request review comment** (add `issues`, `pull_request`, or check/workflow events only if you wire up those hooks).
5. Install the App on the repositories it should watch.

An issue or PR comment that `@mention`s the App's slug starts a turn.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_ID
secrets.description: The GitHub App's numeric App ID.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: The GitHub App's private key (full PEM contents) — used to authenticate as the app and post replies.

secrets.name: GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET
secrets.description: The webhook secret configured on the App — eve verifies the inbound webhook signature against it.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_SLUG
secrets.description: The App's slug — the @name mentioned to trigger the agent.
